extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: Small (edit workbook + update memo + run a local openpyxl check) - estimated 1-2 developer hours.
extra_model_fields.expected_benefit: Reduces reviewer friction, removes ambiguity about formula denominators and tie-breaks, and enables automated parsing in environments with openpyxl.
post_revision_checks: Open the revised .xlsx in Excel/LibreOffice and confirm 'ReviewerChecks' cell addresses match live formulas.
Run openpyxl-based script to read the workbook and confirm weighted_scores reproduce the memo table.
Verify sensitivity worksheet produces the same qualitative statement about 5% shifts as the memo.
extra_model_fields.verifier_recommendation: After these fixes, re-run deterministic check and request a human verifier to open the workbook to confirm cell-level formula denominators and tie-break logic.

planned_changes: Add a new worksheet named 'ReviewerChecks' that lists: each embedded maximum cell address, the formula used for normalization (cell formula text), and the cell addresses for weighted_score and rank outputs.
Implement and surface explicit tie-break formula(s) in the workbook; document the deterministic rule and point to the exact formula cell in 'ReviewerChecks'.
Wrap all normalization denominators with division-by-zero guards (IF or equivalent) and annotate the guards in the reviewer sheet.
Add a 'Sensitivity' worksheet that computes the 5% up/down perturbation per numeric input for each record and shows resulting weighted_scores and rank changes; summarize results in the memo.

Include the python recomputation snippet as text on a 'Reproducibility' sheet, plus the recomputed maxima and cached weighted_scores used in the memo (so reviewers can cross-check without running the trace externally).
Ensure the .xlsx contains no macros and uses standard OOXML formula constructs; re-save with Excel/LibreOffice if needed to maximize openpyxl compatibility.
Update trading_memo.md to cite the exact workbook cell addresses for all cited numbers and add a short 'How to validate' checklist referencing the 'ReviewerChecks' and 'Sensitivity' sheets.
Run a local verification using openpyxl (or Excel) and attach a short verification note (environment and library versions) to the revised submission.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve reviewer reproducibility and remove ambiguity from workbook formulas and memo so the file can be validated in environments that support openpyxl/Excel and to document tie-break and division-by-zero handling.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt meets the artifact contract and the memo reproduces the weighted scores and recommendations. Grader and verifier both passed the deliverable. The primary shortcoming is an environment parsing limitation (openpyxl unavailable) which prevents automated cell-level verification. Recommend local, targeted edits to the workbook and memo to make cell-level logic, tie-breaks, and sensitivity checks explicit and reviewer-friendly.
